cards.back: Cisco Discovery Protocol — Cisco-proprietary L2 protocol. Each Cisco device sends multicast hellos every 60s announcing itself; recipients learn neighbor's hostname, platform, IP, capabilities, port ID, IOS version.

cards.front: Cisco Discovery Protocol — Cisco-proprietary L2 protocol. Each Cisco device sends multicast hellos every 60s announcing itself; recipients learn neighbor's hostname, platform, IP, capabilities, port ID, IOS version.

cards.back: Link Layer Discovery Protocol (IEEE 802.1AB) — vendor-neutral equivalent of CDP. Same purpose, works across vendors. Off by default on Cisco IOS.

cards.front: Link Layer Discovery Protocol (IEEE 802.1AB) — vendor-neutral equivalent of CDP. Same purpose, works across vendors. Off by default on Cisco IOS.

CDP timer defaults: hello = [...] seconds, hold time = 180 seconds.
LLDP timer defaults: hello = 30 seconds, hold time = 120 seconds.

LLDP timer defaults: hello = 30 seconds, hold time = 120 seconds.

LLDP timer defaults: hello = 30 seconds, hold time = 120 seconds.

CDP timer defaults: hello = [...] seconds, hold time = 180 seconds.
LLDP timer defaults: hello = 30 seconds, hold time = 120 seconds.

cards.back:
```
conf t
cdp run                                   ! global on
interface g0/1
 cdp enable                                ! per-interface (default on)
 no cdp enable                             ! disable per-interface
```

```
conf t
lldp run                                   ! global on
interface g0/1
 lldp transmit
 lldp receive
```
LLDP needs both transmit + receive — they're separate.

```
show cdp neighbors
```
Columns: Device ID, Local Intrfce, Holdtme, Capability, Platform, Port ID.

```
show cdp neighbors detail
show cdp entry *                          ! same as detail
```
Adds: management IP, IOS version, native VLAN, duplex.

```
show lldp neighbors                       ! brief
show lldp neighbors detail                ! full info
show lldp                                  ! global timers
show lldp interface g0/1                  ! per-port
```

They leak topology info (device names, IPs, IOS versions, port mappings). On user-facing access ports, **disable** to avoid info leak to attackers. Keep enabled on infrastructure ports (between switches/routers/IP phones).

cards.front: How does an IP phone use CDP/LLDP-MED?
cards.back: Phone listens for CDP-VoVLAN or LLDP-MED messages from switch to learn its voice VLAN ID, QoS settings, PoE power policy. No manual phone config needed.
